meta.name: self-evolution-eval
meta.experiment: 8-6 Design Evaluation Dataset for Self-Evolving Agent
design_principles: Task description only states the goal, without hinting at tool names (e.g., 'Get the English subtitles of a YouTube video' instead of 'Use youtube-transcript-api')
Cover 20 different domains to prevent the Agent from memorizing fixed tool patterns
Each task includes a reference solution (recommended open-source library + typical API example) and known pitfalls (deprecated libraries / APIs requiring payment or registration)
correctness_criteria is used for first-layer task correctness judgment; discovery_keywords is used for second-layer tool discovery effectiveness judgment
tool_name / variant_goal is used for fourth-layer tool reuse capability testing (a second similar task should directly retrieve existing tools)
mock_answer is only used to drive the 'controllable reference Agent' through the harness; the actual tested Agent does not rely on it

tasks.goal: Given a YouTube video link, retrieve the full English subtitle text of that video.
tasks.variant_goal: Given another YouTube video link, also retrieve its English subtitle text.

reference_solution.api_example: from youtube_transcript_api import YouTubeTranscriptApi; text = ' '.join(x['text'] for x in YouTubeTranscriptApi.get_transcript(video_id, languages=['en']))

deprecated_libraries: pytube (subtitle/caption feature has been broken for a long time)
paid_or_registration_apis: YouTube Data API v3 (requires Google Cloud project, quota, and OAuth)
known_pitfalls.notes: video_id needs to be parsed from the URL; some videos with disabled subtitles will throw exceptions, which need to be caught and handled.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should include the retrieved subtitle text or clearly state the source method.

tasks.mock_answer: The English subtitles of this video have been retrieved via youtube_transcript_api, totaling 1240 words, starting with: 'Welcome back to the channel today we...' (transcript text has been obtained).

tasks.goal: Query the current real-time price of Bitcoin (USD) and provide the price trend data for the last 7 days.
tasks.variant_goal: Query the current real-time price of Ethereum and the trend for the last 7 days.

reference_solution.api_example: GET https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/coins/bitcoin/market_chart?vs_currency=usd&days=7 (no API Key required)

deprecated_libraries: cryptocompare (free tier tightened, requires registration for Key)
paid_or_registration_apis: Some Binance endpoints have regional restrictions
known_pitfalls.notes: CoinGecko public API has rate limits; 429 errors need to be handled.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the Bitcoin price in USD and mention the 7-day trend.

tasks.mock_answer: Queried via CoinGecko public API: Bitcoin current price approximately $63,120 USD; over the last 7 days, it rose from $59,800 to $63,120, an increase of about 5.6%, showing an upward trend.

tasks.goal: Find a numerical root of the equation x^3 - 2x - 5 = 0 in the real domain, accurate to 6 decimal places.
tasks.variant_goal: Then find a numerical root of the equation cos(x) - x = 0.

reference_solution.api_example: from scipy.optimize import brentq; root = brentq(lambda x: x**3 - 2*x - 5, 1, 3)

known_pitfalls.notes: Using eval to directly execute user expressions poses a security risk; the root-finding interval should be constrained or Newton's method with symbolic differentiation should be used.

correctness_criteria.description: The real root of this equation is approximately 2.094551.

tasks.mock_answer: Using scipy.optimize.brentq on the interval [1,3] yields the root x ≈ 2.094551.

tasks.goal: Parse the address 'Tiananmen Square, Beijing' into latitude and longitude coordinates.
tasks.variant_goal: Parse the address 'The Bund, Shanghai' into latitude and longitude coordinates.

reference_solution.api_example: from geopy.geocoders import Nominatim; loc = Nominatim(user_agent='demo').geocode('Tiananmen Square, Beijing'); (loc.latitude, loc.longitude)

paid_or_registration_apis: Google Maps Geocoding API (requires billing API Key)
known_pitfalls.notes: Nominatim usage policy requires setting a user_agent and at most 1 request per second.

correctness_criteria.description: Tiananmen Square latitude approximately 39.90, longitude approximately 116.39.

tasks.mock_answer: Using geopy's Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) geocoding: Tiananmen Square coordinates approximately latitude 39.9055, longitude 116.3976.

tasks.goal: Get the current hot post titles (top 10) of a public Reddit subreddit (e.g., r/python).
tasks.variant_goal: Get the top 10 hot post titles of the r/MachineLearning subreddit.

reference_solution.api_example: GET https://www.reddit.com/r/python/hot.json?limit=10 with a custom User-Agent; or use praw (requires registering a script app)

paid_or_registration_apis: Reddit official API now requires OAuth app registration
known_pitfalls.notes: The anonymous .json endpoint is still usable but must include a User-Agent, otherwise rate-limited or banned.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should list the hot post titles of that subreddit.

tasks.mock_answer: Fetch the top 10 hot titles via Reddit's r/python/hot.json endpoint (with User-Agent), e.g., 'What are you working on this week?', 'Async in Python explained', etc.

tasks.goal: Subscribe to an MQTT topic (e.g., sensor/temperature), receive one message, and read the temperature value from it.
tasks.variant_goal: Subscribe to MQTT topic sensor/humidity and read the humidity value from one message.

reference_solution.api_example: import paho.mqtt.client as mqtt; c=mqtt.Client(); c.on_message=cb; c.connect('test.mosquitto.org',1883); c.subscribe('sensor/temperature'); c.loop_start()

paid_or_registration_apis: Partially managed MQTT brokers (e.g., HiveMQ Cloud) require credential registration
known_pitfalls.notes: Should use a public test broker (e.g., test.mosquitto.org); set a timeout to avoid permanent blocking.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should explain subscribing via MQTT and reading the temperature value.

tasks.mock_answer: Use paho-mqtt to connect to test.mosquitto.org:1883, subscribe to sensor/temperature, receive a message with payload='23.5', parse to get temperature 23.5°C.

tasks.goal: Get the weather forecast (max/min temperature) for a city (e.g., Tokyo) for the next 3 days.
tasks.variant_goal: Get the weather forecast for London for the next 3 days.

reference_solution.api_example: GET https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=35.68&longitude=139.69&daily=temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min&forecast_days=3 (free, no key required)

deprecated_libraries: Dark Sky API (acquired and shut down by Apple)
paid_or_registration_apis: OpenWeatherMap free tier requires API key registration and has call limits
known_pitfalls.notes: Open-Meteo is free, no key required, and is the most convenient choice.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the high and low temperature forecast for the next 3 days.

tasks.mock_answer: Get Tokyo's 3-day forecast via Open-Meteo free API: Day1 max 28°C min 21°C; Day2 27°C/20°C; Day3 29°C/22°C.

tasks.goal: Determine which natural language a given text belongs to (language detection).
tasks.variant_goal: Perform language detection on another piece of text.

reference_solution.api_example: from langdetect import detect, DetectorFactory; DetectorFactory.seed=0; detect('Bonjour tout le monde')  # -> 'fr'

known_pitfalls.notes: langdetect results are uncertain; need to set DetectorFactory.seed for reproducibility.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the detected language.

tasks.mock_answer: Using langdetect (seed=0 set for reproducibility), the text 'Bonjour tout le monde' is detected as French (fr).

tasks.goal: Read EXIF metadata from a JPEG photo, extract the capture time and camera model.
tasks.variant_goal: Read EXIF from another photo, extract the capture time and GPS coordinates.

reference_solution.api_example: from PIL import Image; from PIL.ExifTags import TAGS; exif={TAGS.get(k,k):v for k,v in Image.open('p.jpg')._getexif().items()}

known_pitfalls.notes: Images without EXIF: _getexif() returns None, need to check for None; GPS requires additional parsing with GPSTAGS.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should provide the shooting time/camera info from EXIF.

tasks.mock_answer: Parse EXIF with Pillow's ExifTags: DateTimeOriginal=2023:08:12 14:05:33, camera model Model=Canon EOS R6.

tasks.goal: Extract plain text content from all pages of a PDF file.
tasks.variant_goal: Extract plain text from another PDF file.

reference_solution.api_example: from pypdf import PdfReader; text='\n'.join(p.extract_text() or '' for p in PdfReader('a.pdf').pages)

deprecated_libraries: PyPDF2 (merged and renamed to pypdf, old package discontinued)
paid_or_registration_apis: Adobe PDF Extract API (requires registration credentials)
known_pitfalls.notes: Scanned PDFs require OCR (pytesseract); plain text extraction yields empty strings.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should state that text was extracted using pypdf/pdfplumber.

tasks.mock_answer: Using pypdf (modern replacement for PyPDF2) to call extract_text() page by page, extracted 12 pages of text, totaling about 8,300 characters.

tasks.goal: Calculate sunrise and sunset times for a location (e.g., Beijing, latitude 39.9, longitude 116.4) on a given date.
tasks.variant_goal: Calculate sunrise and sunset times for Shanghai on a given date.

reference_solution.api_example: from astral import LocationInfo; from astral.sun import sun; s=sun(LocationInfo('Beijing','China','Asia/Shanghai',39.9,116.4).observer, date=d)

deprecated_libraries: pyephem (renamed to ephem, old name no longer updated)
known_pitfalls.notes: Note timezone handling: astral returns UTC, need to convert to local timezone.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should provide sunrise and sunset times.

tasks.mock_answer: Using astral to calculate for Beijing on a given day: sunrise about 05:12, sunset about 19:38 (local timezone Asia/Shanghai).

tasks.goal: Calculate the molecular weight of a molecular formula (e.g., C6H12O6).
tasks.variant_goal: Calculate the molecular weight of C8H10N4O2 (caffeine).

reference_solution.api_example: from molmass import Formula; Formula('C6H12O6').mass  # -> 180.156

known_pitfalls.notes: rdkit installation is heavy (recommend conda); molmass/periodictable are lighter pure Python options.

correctness_criteria.description: Glucose C6H12O6 molecular weight is about 180.16 g/mol.

tasks.mock_answer: Using molmass to calculate: Formula('C6H12O6').mass ≈ 180.16 g/mol.

tasks.goal: Given a DNA sequence, calculate its GC content percentage and translate it into the corresponding protein sequence.
tasks.variant_goal: Given another DNA sequence, calculate GC content and translate into protein.

reference_solution.api_example: from Bio.Seq import Seq; from Bio.SeqUtils import gc_fraction; s=Seq('ATGGCC...'); gc_fraction(s); s.translate()

deprecated_libraries: Bio.SeqUtils.GC (renamed to gc_fraction in new version, old function deprecated)
known_pitfalls.notes: Sequence length must be a multiple of 3 for clean translation, otherwise trailing residues are discarded with a warning.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should provide GC content and the translated protein sequence.

tasks.mock_answer: Using Biopython: sequence GC content ≈ 58.3%, translate() yields protein sequence 'MAIVMGR*'.

tasks.goal: Read the duration (seconds) and sample rate of an audio file (mp3/wav).
tasks.variant_goal: Read the duration and sample rate of another audio file.

reference_solution.api_example: from mutagen import File; f=File('a.mp3'); f.info.length; f.info.sample_rate

known_pitfalls.notes: librosa has heavy dependencies (numba/llvmlite); for metadata only, mutagen/soundfile is lighter.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should provide duration and sample rate.

tasks.mock_answer: Using mutagen to read a.mp3: duration ≈ 213.4 seconds, sample_rate = 44100 Hz.

tasks.goal: Get the current real-time exchange rate from USD to CNY.
tasks.variant_goal: Get the current real-time exchange rate from EUR to JPY.

reference_solution.api_example: GET https://api.frankfurter.app/latest?from=USD&to=CNY (free, no key required); or https://open.er-api.com/v6/latest/USD

paid_or_registration_apis: fixer.io free tier requires a key and only supports EUR as base
known_pitfalls.notes: frankfurter.app data source is the European Central Bank, includes only fiat currencies, updated each business day.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the USD to CNY exchange rate value.

tasks.mock_answer: Query via frankfurter.app free API: 1 USD ≈ 7.18 CNY (data source European Central Bank, updated every working day).

tasks.goal: Get the daily closing price of a stock (e.g., AAPL) for the last month.
tasks.variant_goal: Get the daily closing price of MSFT for the last month.

reference_solution.api_example: import yfinance as yf; df = yf.Ticker('AAPL').history(period='1mo'); df['Close']

paid_or_registration_apis: Alpha Vantage free Key only 5 requests per minute
known_pitfalls.notes: yfinance is unofficial scraping, occasional rate limiting; need to handle empty DataFrame.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the stock's closing price series for the last month.

tasks.mock_answer: Use yfinance to get AAPL history with period='1mo', obtaining 22 trading days' closing prices, e.g., most recent day Close=214.29.

tasks.goal: Calculate the spherical (great-circle) distance between two sets of latitude/longitude coordinates, in kilometers.
tasks.variant_goal: Calculate the great-circle distance between another two sets of latitude/longitude coordinates.

reference_solution.api_example: from geopy.distance import geodesic; geodesic((39.9,116.4),(31.2,121.5)).km

known_pitfalls.notes: When writing haversine manually, note converting angles to radians and Earth radius as 6371 km; geodesic is more accurate (ellipsoid model).

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the distance in kilometers between the two points.

tasks.mock_answer: Use geopy.distance.geodesic to calculate the distance between Beijing (39.9,116.4) and Shanghai (31.2,121.5) is approximately 1067.6 km.

tasks.goal: Parse an RSS/Atom feed and extract the titles and links of the latest 5 entries.
tasks.variant_goal: Parse another RSS feed and extract the latest 5 titles and links.

reference_solution.api_example: import feedparser; d=feedparser.parse(url); [(e.title,e.link) for e in d.entries[:5]]

known_pitfalls.notes: feedparser is tolerant of malformed XML; need to check d.bozo to determine if parsing had warnings.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the titles of the latest entries from the feed.

tasks.mock_answer: Use feedparser to parse the RSS feed, retrieve the latest 5 entries, e.g., '(title) OpenAI releases ...' -> (link) https://... .

tasks.goal: Generate a QR code image containing specified text (e.g., a URL) and save it as PNG.
tasks.variant_goal: Generate a QR code PNG for another piece of text.

reference_solution.api_example: import qrcode; qrcode.make('https://example.com').save('out.png')  # requires pillow

paid_or_registration_apis: Various online QR code generation APIs (some limited/require key)
known_pitfalls.notes: The qrcode library requires pillow to save PNG; segno has no extra dependencies.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should indicate that a QR code PNG has been generated.

tasks.mock_answer: Using the qrcode library: qrcode.make('https://example.com').save('out.png'), a 290x290 QR code PNG has been generated.

tasks.goal: Convert a given local time from New York timezone to Tokyo timezone, correctly handling daylight saving time.
tasks.variant_goal: Convert a London time to Shanghai timezone.

reference_solution.api_example: from datetime import datetime; from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo; dt.replace(tzinfo=ZoneInfo('America/New_York')).astimezone(ZoneInfo('Asia/Tokyo'))

deprecated_libraries: Using datetime.timezone with fixed offset directly (cannot handle DST)
known_pitfalls.notes: pytz requires localize() instead of direct replace(), otherwise it will carry historical LMT offset; prefer zoneinfo for Py3.9+.

correctness_criteria.description: The final answer should give the converted Tokyo time.

tasks.mock_answer: Using the standard library zoneinfo: New York 2024-06-01 09:00 (America/New_York, EDT) converts to Tokyo time 2024-06-01 22:00 (Asia/Tokyo), correctly handling DST.
